title: ITR 2026: Senior citizens can claim ₹50,000 deduction even without health insurance —Here's how
summary: The income tax return filing deadline is approaching, prompting taxpayers to gather documents. Section 80D allows senior citizens without health insurance to claim up to ₹50,000 for medical expenses, available only under the old tax regime.

title: Bought US startup shares using overseas savings? Here's how India will tax it
summary: A returning NRI who invested overseas savings in a US startup may not face tax on the purchase itself, but discounted acquisitions and annual foreign asset disclosures can trigger tax and compliance obligations.

title: EPFO: Taxation rule on Voluntary Provident Fund — the ₹2.5 lakh VPF threshold, explained
summary: VPF allows employees to contribute more than the mandatory EPF rate for a larger retirement corpus. Contributions over ₹2.5 lakh face taxation on excess interest. VPF matches EPF's interest rate and qualifies for tax deductions under Section 80C, with specific withdrawal conditions.

title: ₹17L tax refund denied after taxpayer missed ITR e-verification while caring for ill father: Here's what ITAT ruled
summary: A Delhi taxpayer was denied a ₹17 lakh tax refund due to the late e-verification of his ITR while caring for his ill father. ITAT ruled that denying the refund on technical grounds violates Article 265 of the Constitution, leading to a ruling in his favour.
